creator: philip@FreeBSD.org
text:
On 2007-02-14 10:28:30 (+0000), Anton A. Vesnin <freeman@bsd-admin.org> wrote:
> psm0: current command byte:0065
> psm0: <PS/2 Mouse> irq 12 on atkbdc0
> ioapic0: routing intpin 12 (ISA IRQ 12) to vector 60
> psm0: [GIANT-LOCKED]
> psm0: model GlidePoint, device ID 0-00, 2 buttons
> psm0: config:00000000, flags:00000008, packet size:3
> psm0: syncmask:c0, syncbits:00

Are you sure this is a Synaptics?  The driver seems to believe it's a
GlidePoint.

If it's a Synaptics, could you please build a kernel with PSM_DEBUG=2 and show
me the more verbose messages from the driver?  Alternatively, you can set the
debug option with a tunable at boot-time, but I forgot what I called it. :-)

Regards,

 - Philip

-- 
Philip Paeps                                             My opinions are
philip@freebsd.org

  Any child who chatters non-stop at home will adamantly
  refuse to utter a word when requested to demonstrate
  for an audience.
